title: LmuxConfig
description: User configuration for vmux (vmux-tui). Written to the XDG config path (typically ~/.config/vmux/config.json) via `vmux config set` or the attach Settings panel.

sidebar_width.description: Expanded sidebar width in terminal columns. When sidebar_fit is true this is the maximum width.

sidebar_fit.description: Fit sidebar width to workspace name text (up to sidebar_width).

prefix_key.description: Prefix chord for attach UI keybindings (must parse as a key binding).

scroll_step.description: Lines per scroll step in the attach UI.

scrollback_bytes.description: Bytes of raw output retained per pane for scrollback and persistence. Takes effect on the next daemon start.

theme.description: Color theme for the attach UI.

workspace_second_line.description: What the second line of each workspace row in the sidebar shows.

cursor_blink.description: Soft-blink the active pane caret while idle.

cursor_blink_ms.description: Half-period of the caret blink in milliseconds (on or off duration).

resume_agents.description: On daemon restart, relaunch agent CLIs resuming their hook-reported conversation (claude --resume <id>) instead of a blank chat.

status_markers.description: How agent status markers render in the sidebar.

default_shell.description: Shell binary name or path when a pane command is empty. Empty uses $SHELL.

default_cwd.description: Default working directory for new workspaces/panes: launch cwd or $HOME.

mouse.description: Capture mouse in the attach UI (click, drag, wheel).

tab_close_button.description: Show × on workspace tabs when more than one tab exists.

bell_on_attention.description: Terminal bell when a pane enters attention / needs-input.

sidebar_responsive.description: Auto-hide the workspace sidebar on narrow terminals (burger + picker).

relay.description: Mobile / Cmux Remote-compatible phone relay preferences.

enabled.description: When true, vmux attach starts the phone relay if it is not already up.

bind.description: Where the relay listens. Never binds 0.0.0.0. auto: Tailscale IP if online else localhost; tailscale: Tailscale only; local: 127.0.0.1 only.

port.description: TCP port for the phone relay (default 4399, Cmux Remote default). Override with config or `vmux relay serve --listen host:port`.

allow_localhost.description: Allow localhost device registration (dev / same machine).

allow_tailnet_cgnat.description: Accept Tailscale CGNAT peers without whois (weaker; practical when whois is unavailable).

allow_paste.description: Serve the browser paste page (GET /paste, POST /v1/paste). Uploads still require a paired device token.

allow_view_resize.description: Let phone viewers shrink panes to fit their screen (leased view-size overrides).

agent_titles.description: Automatic tab naming for panes running coding agents.

enabled.description: Master switch for automatic tab titles.

llm_fallback.description: Ask llm_command to name the tab when no free source produced a title.

llm_command.description: Headless command that reads a prompt on stdin and prints a short title.

llm_delay_ms.description: How long an agent pane may run without a usable title before falling back to llm_command.

ports.description: Workspace listening-port detection and optional local forwarding helpers.

enabled.description: Detect listening ports owned by processes under workspace panes (via ss).

notify.description: How to surface newly detected ports in the attach UI.

auto_forward.description: Automatically start a forward when a new matching port appears.

forward_via.description: Preferred path when auto_forward is on or when the UI offers forward.

poll_secs.description: How often the background meta loop rescans listening ports.

ignore.description: Port numbers to never surface or auto-forward.

ignore_processes.description: Process names (comm) to ignore when attributing listening ports.

ignore_ephemeral.description: Hide high-numbered ephemeral listeners that are usually not app servers.

ssh_host.description: Default SSH target for `vmux ports ssh-cmd` / SSH-based forwards (e.g. user@host).
